title: EDR compatibility and supported scope
text: Discovery tables (edr_collections(), edr_parameters(), edr_instances()) return tibbles stable scalar columns list columns metadata flattened without loss. Raw collection/instance documents remain available raw list column. Detailed discovery calls return parsed JSON list. CoverageJSON query verbs return edr_response; convert explicitly covjson_to_tibble(). GeoJSON collection verbs return sf sf installed otherwise retain edr_response. CSV responses return tibble. HTTP 204 produces typed empty result rather parser failure. numeric CoverageJSON ranges, values serialized JSON strings coerced every non-missing string finite safely representable; actual text, overflow/underflow, unsafe declared integers remain explicit parser errors. edr_location_batch() returns edr_location_batch object three stable tibbles: requests (one status row per planned location/window query), data (row-bound CoverageJSON/CSV data .request_id .location_id provenance), errors (preserved conditions on_error = "collect"). Optional day/week/month/year chunks require finite closed interval. Adjacent windows share boundary; exact rows repeated across windows location removed default, requests$n_rows retains raw response counts. Timestamp bounds normalized UTC calendar arithmetic may contain six fractional-second digits; values R represent without loss rejected requests begin. sequential release normal client retry policy remains authoritative. Pagination deliberately opt-. client follows server-advertised body link rel = "next", treats cursor/offset opaque, requires continuation remain original request’s service origin. Reaching max_pages max_features another page exists typed error, silently partial result. edr_explore() enable collection-wide pagination automatically. Local argument parser failures raised issuing unsafe follow-requests. HTTP failures retain httr2_http_* status classes include useful server error body one available. edr_diagnose() exception: validating arguments, records metadata/network failures fail, warn, skip rows one broken discovery endpoint hide rest report.

title: EDR compatibility and supported scope
text: 0.2 discovery layer retains query-specific formats, units, CRS details, extents, parameter metadata instead flattening away. Discovery responses cached per client bounded period. Use refresh = TRUE discovery call current server state matters, clear client’s metadata edr_cache_clear(client).

title: Layering EDR data: Lake Mead

title: Layering EDR data: Lake Mead
text: population collection requires explicit CRS area query. four-row matrix closed WKT polygon edr_area(). result regular 90 66 grid 5,940 cells. edr_map() handles cell bounds, palette, legend, map extent directly tidy rows.
code: population_ring <- rbind(   c(study_bbox[1], study_bbox[2]),   c(study_bbox[3], study_bbox[2]),   c(study_bbox[3], study_bbox[4]),   c(study_bbox[1], study_bbox[4]) )  population_response <- edr_area(   met,   "global_pop_density",   coords = population_ring,   parameter_name = "Pop_Density",   crs = "EPSG:4326" )  population <- covjson_to_tibble(population_response) population$parameter_code <- population$parameter population$parameter <- "2015 population density" population$unit <- "people/km2"  stopifnot(   nrow(population) > 0L,   all(c("x", "y", "value") %in% names(population)),   any(is.finite(population$value), na.rm = TRUE) )

title: Layering EDR data: Lake Mead
text: Two USGS gauges provide stage series Hoover Dam discharge series Las Vegas Wash. USGS currently ignores datetime individual location requests, example asks 31 records uses dates actually returned. WWDH treats interval end exclusive, adding one day includes last USGS date. Location 3514 USBR/RISE Lake Mead storage series. WWDH location index currently ignores spatial/page limits, USBR marker constructed coordinates already carried returned coverage instead downloading full index.
code: usgs_index <- edr_locations(   usgs,   "daily-edr",   bbox = study_bbox,   limit = 100 )  selected_usgs_ids <- c("USGS-09421500", "USGS-09419800") usgs_sites <- usgs_index[   match(selected_usgs_ids, usgs_index$id),   c("id", "monitoring_location_name", "geometry") ]  hoover_response <- edr_location(   usgs,   "daily-edr",   location_id = "USGS-09421500",   parameter_name = "00065",   limit = 31 ) hoover_stage <- covjson_to_tibble(hoover_response) hoover_stage <- hoover_stage[order(hoover_stage$datetime), ] hoover_stage$parameter_code <- hoover_stage$parameter hoover_stage$parameter <- "Gage height" hoover_stage$coverage_id <- paste0("usgs:", hoover_stage$coverage_id)  wash_response <- edr_location(   usgs,   "daily-edr",   location_id = "USGS-09419800",   parameter_name = "00060",   limit = 31 ) wash_flow <- covjson_to_tibble(wash_response) wash_flow <- wash_flow[order(wash_flow$datetime), ] wash_flow$parameter_code <- wash_flow$parameter wash_flow$parameter <- "Discharge" wash_flow$coverage_id <- paste0("usgs:", wash_flow$coverage_id)  water_dates <- intersect(   as.Date(hoover_stage$datetime, tz = "UTC"),   as.Date(wash_flow$datetime, tz = "UTC") ) stopifnot(length(water_dates) > 0L) water_interval <- paste(   min(water_dates),   max(water_dates) + 1,   sep = "/" )  storage_response <- edr_location(   wwdh,   "rise-edr",   location_id = "3514",   datetime = water_interval,   parameter_name = "3" ) storage <- covjson_to_tibble(storage_response) storage <- storage[order(storage$datetime), ] storage <- storage[   as.Date(storage$datetime, tz = "UTC") %in% water_dates, ] storage$parameter_code <- storage$parameter storage$parameter <- "Reservoir storage" storage$coverage_id <- paste0("wwdh:", storage$coverage_id)  stopifnot(   nrow(hoover_stage) > 0L,   nrow(wash_flow) > 0L,   nrow(storage) > 0L,   all(vapply(     list(hoover_stage, wash_flow, storage),     function(x) all(c("datetime", "value", "unit") %in% names(x)),     logical(1)   )) ) usbr_site <- sf::st_as_sf(   data.frame(     id = "USBR-RISE-3514",     monitoring_location_name =       "Lake Mead Hoover Dam and Powerplant (USBR via WWDH/RISE)",     longitude = storage$x[[1]],     latitude = storage$y[[1]]   ),   coords = c("longitude", "latitude"),   crs = 4326 )  usgs_popup_data <- list(   "USGS-09421500" = hoover_stage,   "USGS-09419800" = wash_flow ) usbr_popup_data <- list(   "USBR-RISE-3514" = storage )

text: map combines one gridded coverage three temporal point coverages. container domain metadata come directly responses:
code: coverage_signature <- function(source, response) {   body <- response$covjson   coverages <- if (identical(body$type, "CoverageCollection")) {     body$coverages   } else {     list(body)   }    domain_types <- vapply(coverages, function(coverage) {     domain_type <- coverage$domainType     if (is.null(domain_type) || length(domain_type) == 0L) {       domain_type <- coverage$domain$domainType     }     if (is.null(domain_type) || length(domain_type) == 0L) {       "not declared"     } else {       as.character(domain_type[[1]])     }   }, character(1))    axes <- unique(unlist(lapply(     coverages,     function(coverage) names(coverage$domain$axes)   )))    data.frame(     source = source,     container = body$type,     domain = paste(unique(domain_types), collapse = ", "),     axes = paste(axes, collapse = ", "),     coverages = length(coverages)   ) }  signatures <- rbind(   coverage_signature("Met Office population", population_response),   coverage_signature("USGS Hoover stage", hoover_response),   coverage_signature("USGS Las Vegas Wash", wash_response),   coverage_signature("WWDH / USBR storage", storage_response) )  knitr::kable(signatures) snapshot <- data.frame(   layer = c(     "Met Office population",     "USGS Hoover stage",     "USGS Las Vegas Wash",     "WWDH / USBR storage"   ),   rows = c(     nrow(population),     nrow(hoover_stage),     nrow(wash_flow),     nrow(storage)   ),   start = c(     "static 2015",     format(min(hoover_stage$datetime), "%Y-%m-%d", tz = "UTC"),     format(min(wash_flow$datetime), "%Y-%m-%d", tz = "UTC"),     format(min(storage$datetime), "%Y-%m-%d", tz = "UTC")   ),   end = c(     "static 2015",     format(max(hoover_stage$datetime), "%Y-%m-%d", tz = "UTC"),     format(max(wash_flow$datetime), "%Y-%m-%d", tz = "UTC"),     format(max(storage$datetime), "%Y-%m-%d", tz = "UTC")   ),   unit = c(     "people/km2",     unique(hoover_stage$unit)[1],     unique(wash_flow$unit)[1],     unique(storage$unit)[1]   ) )  knitr::kable(snapshot)

title: USGS streamgages: bounded discovery and mapping

title: USGS streamgages: bounded discovery and mapping
text: USGS quirk flag front: ask specific station edr_location(), server ignores datetime interval just returns recent limit records (limit defaults 10). get five months daily data, pass limit comfortably covers window – say 200 days – filter target window client-side. edr_plot() small ggplot wrapper tidy tibble: plot chunk plot-one-gauge already know station IDs, public batch helper performs bounded per-station workflow without discovering parallelizing anything: USGS beta currently ignores datetime individual location queries returns latest records. add chunk expecting historical windows: chunking intended EDR implementations honor requested interval.
code: example_id   <- gauges$id[[1]] example_name <- gauges$monitoring_location_name[[1]]  resp <- edr_location(   usgs, "daily-edr",   location_id    = example_id,   parameter_name = "00060",   limit          = 200 ) df <- covjson_to_tibble(resp)  # Filter to the Jan–May 2026 window we care about df_jan_may <- df[df$datetime >= as.POSIXct("2026-01-01", tz = "UTC") &                  df$datetime <= as.POSIXct("2026-05-31", tz = "UTC"), ] head(df_jan_may[, c("datetime", "value", "unit")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   datetime            value unit  #>   <dttm>              <dbl> <chr> #> 1 2026-05-31 00:00:00  2.21 ft3/s #> 2 2026-05-30 00:00:00  2.53 ft3/s #> 3 2026-05-29 00:00:00  2.66 ft3/s #> 4 2026-05-28 00:00:00  3.14 ft3/s #> 5 2026-05-27 00:00:00  3.13 ft3/s #> 6 2026-05-26 00:00:00  3.29 ft3/s edr_plot(df_jan_may) +   ggtitle(paste0(example_name, " — daily streamflow")) all_streamflow <- edr_location_batch(   usgs, "daily-edr",   location_id    = gauges$id,   parameter_name = "00060",   limit           = 200,   max_requests    = nrow(gauges),   on_error        = "collect",   progress        = FALSE )  all_streamflow$requests all_streamflow$data all_streamflow$errors

what: A few things worth knowing about the USGS endpoint
title: USGS streamgages: bounded discovery and mapping
text: daily-edr advertises locations query. EDR verbs (cube, area, position, …) return HTTP errors. per-station fallback works fine tens gauges; hundreds consider pre-filtering aggressively bbox =. datetime honoured /locations/{id} requests – server returns latest limit records regardless. Pass enough limit cover window, filter client-side. station id format "USGS-<NWIS-id>". bare NWIS id ("02087500") doesn’t work; pass full "USGS-..." form, edr_locations() returns id column. 44-column attribute table gauges carries lot useful metadata (drainage_area, state_name, hydrologic_unit_code, altitude, …). Use filter label mapping. Pass label_col = "monitoring_location_name" edr_map() popup titles read “BUFFALO CREEK NEAR FALLS, NC” instead raw USGS-02087183 id.

what: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: R client OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR) services expose JSON discovery metadata CoverageJSON, GeoJSON, CSV query responses. spec general, practice package gets use -situ monitoring networks — stream gauges, weather stations, snow telemetry, reservoir telemetry — expose stations time series EDR. Two known-good places point : USGS waterdata OGC API — stream gauges water-quality stations U.S. Geological Survey. Western Water Datahub — pygeoapi deployment wraps RISE, SNOTEL, USACE, AWDB monitoring sources behind single EDR endpoint. cross-server experiments, Met Office Labs EDR demonstrator another useful endpoint. technical demonstrator, operational service: availability, collections, response details can change without notice, build production workflows around . cross-endpoint Lake Mead vignette shows 2015 population grid alongside USGS river discharge Western Water Datahub reservoir storage without mixing provenance units. goal take tedious parts EDR hands — URL construction, comma-separated parameter lists, WKT coordinate encoding, retries, content negotiation — hand back something can actually data analysis : CoverageJSON → long tibble (one row per coverage × parameter × time step), via covjson_to_tibble(). GeoJSON → sf object, via geojson_to_sf(). CSV → tibble, parsed directly query helper.

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: Start pointing client server. base URL thing really needs: Collection IDs service-specific. first thing new service run edr_collections() read data_queries column see EDR endpoints collection supports. new unfamiliar implementation, inspect advertised support issuing data queries: edr_supports() reports metadata advertises; FALSE proof partially conformant server handle request. Discovery metadata cached per client short, configurable period. Use refresh = TRUE current server state matters, edr_cache_clear(client) clear explicitly. try non-operational Met Office demonstrator deliberately small request, query one terrain point rather forecast collection: example also exercised scheduled, non-blocking live smoke check; never run part CRAN checks regular test suite. Collections representing model runs may advertise instances. query verbs work instance instance_id named explicitly:
code: library(edr4r)  client <- edr_client("https://api.waterdata.usgs.gov/ogcapi/beta") # or "https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app" # or "http://localhost:5005" if you're running pygeoapi locally  collections <- edr_collections(client) collections[, c("id", "title", "data_queries", "output_formats")] edr_capabilities(client, "daily-edr") edr_supports(client, "daily-edr", query = "locations") edr_diagnose(client, "daily-edr") met <- edr_client(   "https://labs.metoffice.gov.uk/edr",   timeout = 10,   max_tries = 1 )  terrain <- edr_position(   met,   "terrain_tiles",   coords = c(-0.1276, 51.5072),   parameter_name = "Height" ) covjson_to_tibble(terrain) runs <- edr_instances(met, "moglobal-station-level") run_id <- runs$id[[1]]  run_capabilities <- edr_capabilities(   met, "moglobal-station-level", instance_id = run_id ) edr_supports(   run_capabilities, query = "locations" ) run_locations <- edr_locations(   met, "moglobal-station-level",   instance_id = run_id )

what: Pull several station time series safely
title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: already station IDs, edr_location_batch() makes one bounded, sequential edr_location() request per ID keeps request provenance failures visible: helper deliberately discover stations parallelize requests; full request count known validated network activity. server honors bounded datetime intervals, split long pull calendar windows chunk. request cap applies complete station--window plan, ten stations twelve monthly windows requires cap least 120: Adjacent windows share boundary EDR intervals closed server inclusivity varies. default, exact observations repeated across windows station retained earliest request; deduplicate = FALSE preserves raw responses. requests$n_rows records pre-deduplication row count request. USGS beta location endpoint currently ignores datetime returns latest records. Chunking therefore useful endpoints honor requested interval; workaround retrieving USGS history.
code: pull <- edr_location_batch(   client, "daily-values",   location_id    = locs$id[1:10],   datetime       = "2020-01-01/2020-12-31",   parameter_name = "discharge",   max_requests   = 10,   on_error       = "collect" )  pull$data                  # .request_id and .location_id identify every row pull$errors                # typed, empty tibble when every request succeeded pull$requests              # success / empty / error status for every ID wwdh <- edr_client("https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app")  monthly_pull <- edr_location_batch(   wwdh, "rise-edr",   location_id    = "3514",   datetime       = "2023-01-01/2024-01-01",   parameter_name = "3",   chunk           = "1 month",   max_requests    = 12,   on_error        = "collect" )

text: edr_map() puts stations leaflet basemap. Pass data = named list keyed station id (shape [edr_explore()] produces) marker gets popup inline plot “Download CSV” link station’s data — embedded data: URI saved HTML selfcontained: quick exploratory pass whole collection, edr_explore() fetch + plot + map one call: Gridded coverages vertical profiles can mapped . edr_map() detects tidy CoverageJSON grids/profiles puts slice selectors inside leaflet widget multiple parameters datetimes; grids also get z selector multiple vertical levels present: Coverage station layers can share one widget. Start coverage map, add independently styled station groups normal chart/CSV popups: edr_explore() uses behavior bulk coverage queries. Use output = "plot" want ggplot instead interactive map:
code: stations <- edr_locations(client, "monitoring-locations",                           bbox = c(-116, 35.5, -114, 36.5)) data_list <- list("3514" = covjson_to_tibble(resp)) m <- edr_map(stations, data = data_list, popup = "plot+csv") edr_save_html(m, "stations.html") edr_explore(   client, "daily-values",   bbox           = c(-116, 35.5, -114, 36.5),   datetime       = "2024-01-01/2024-03-31",   parameter_name = "discharge",   limit          = 25,   file           = "snapshot.html" ) grid <- covjson_to_tibble(cube) edr_map(grid)  profile <- covjson_to_tibble(profile_resp) edr_map(profile) m <- edr_map(grid, grid_transform = "sqrt") m <- edr_add_stations(   m, stations,   data = data_list,   popup = "plot+csv",   group = "USGS" ) edr_explore(client, "gridded-collection",             bbox = c(-120, 39, -118, 41),             method = "cube")  edr_explore(client, "profile-collection",             coords = c(-119, 40),             method = "position")  edr_explore(client, "profile-collection",             coords = c(-119, 40),             method = "position", output = "plot")

what: Weird IDs, CSV, and an escape hatch
title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: monitoring networks use compound station IDs — colon-separated triplets common pattern. client URL-encodes reserved characters : server advertises CSV, can ask instead CovJSON: need hit endpoint encoding package doesn’t wrap, edr_request() raw escape hatch:
code: edr_location(client, "station-network", "1185:CO:SNTL",              datetime = "2024-01-01/..") edr_location(client, "daily-values", "08313000",              datetime = "2010-01-01/..", format = "csv") service_description <- edr_request(   client, "api", format = "raw", parse = FALSE )

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: Every collection query helper also accepts named instance_id =; set, path becomes /collections/{id}/instances/{instance_id}/{query}. server actually supports varies. Every query verb EDR spec supported client, servers implement subset. -situ monitoring deployments, locations, position, cube, area common; radius, trajectory, corridor less . Hitting verb server doesn’t implement gives HTTP error. Check data_queries column edr_collections() assume query work. See vignette("compatibility") precise supported subset, return formats, known limitations, distinction verified merely advertised endpoint behavior.

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: Every query verb accepts standard EDR filters: datetime — ISO-8601 instant interval. Accepts "2020-01-01/2020-12-31", open interval "2020-01-01/..", length-2 character vector c("2020-01-01", "2020-12-31"). parameter_name — character vector parameter names; sent comma-separated parameter-name= query. Use edr_parameters() discover valid names. bbox — numeric length-4 (minx, miny, maxx, maxy) length-6 (z). coords — position/area/radius/trajectory/corridor: WKT string, numeric vector / 2-column matrix lon-lat, sf/sfc geometry. z, crs, limit — passed supplied. instance_id — named, optional model-run/version identifier; inserts standard /instances/{id} path segment query type. ... — extra query parameter forwarded verbatim.

title: Add station layers to an EDR map — edr_add_stations
text: map leaflet htmlwidget, typically returned edr_map(). locations sf object edr_locations(), edr_response wrapping GeoJSON, GeoJSON FeatureCollection. data Optional station observations: tidy tibble location-id column, named list tibbles keyed feature id, NULL attribute-popups. popup One "plot+csv" (default), "plot", "csv", "table", "". location_col Column data carrying location id data single tibble. Default "coverage_id". id_col Column locations join . NULL, function looks "id" "_id" first character column. label_col Column locations used popup heading. NULL, tries "name", "locationName", "title", detected id column. parameter Optional character vector restricting rows data used popups. Filtered rows also determine whether station marked data. plot_width, plot_height Popup chart dimensions inches. Display size pixels plot_width * plot_dpi plot_height * plot_dpi, larger minimum size readable interactive popups. plot_dpi Display dots-per-inch popup charts. Default 72; bump 90+ popups look small hi-DPI displays. marker_radius Marker radius pixels stations time-series data. Data-less stations drawn one pixel smaller. matched_color Marker colour stations joined coverage data. Default deep blue. unmatched_color Marker colour stations without data (relevant data supplied show_unmatched = TRUE). Default light grey. show_unmatched TRUE (default), data-less stations drawn unmatched_color user can see full station network. Set FALSE drop entirely. Ignored data NULL. legend TRUE (default), add legend distinguishing stations data without. Suppressed automatically unmatched markers label. max_match_distance Optional maximum coordinate distance spatially matching data rows x / y columns stations. Units station coordinates. NULL (default) keeps nearest-station fallback unlimited. group Optional Leaflet layer-group name. default, "Stations", makes added markers available leaflet::addLayersControl(). Pass NULL retain status groups used standalone station map ("data" "data window"). fit TRUE, fit map added station extent. Defaults FALSE adding stations replace existing coverage extent.

title: Create an EDR client — edr_client
text: base_url Base URL OGC API - EDR service. Examples: USGS waterdata OGC API "https://api.waterdata.usgs.gov/ogcapi/beta", Western Water Datahub "https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app", "http://localhost:5005" local pygeoapi dev server. trailing slash optional. user_agent String sent User-Agent header. Defaults "edr4r/<version> (+https://github.com/ksonda/edr4r)". timeout Request timeout seconds. Defaults 60. max_tries Maximum number attempts per request. client retries 408, 429, 5xx responses exponential backoff. Defaults 3. retry_on_failure TRUE (default), retry low-level transport failures connection resets transient DNS / TLS errors. EDR requests made package read-GET requests, retrying safe. cache_ttl Number seconds retain discovery responses client's -memory cache. Defaults 300 (five minutes). Use 0 disable caching Inf retain metadata edr_cache_clear() called. Data-query responses never cached. headers Named character vector extra headers attached every request (e.g. c(Authorization = "Bearer ...")). verbose TRUE, prints request URLs console made. Useful debugging.

text: object class edr_client.

title: Create an EDR client — edr_client

Use edr_parameters() discover valid ids. limit Optional cap number stations map. record_limit Optional per-station record cap, passed edr_location() per-location path. Otherwise return leaflet map. popup Popup mode (forwarded edr_map()). method One "auto" (default), "cube", "area", "position", "per-location". "auto" returns station map station time-series results interactive coverage map gridded/profile results. plot_view Plot view passed edr_plot() returning plot. Defaults "auto". quiet FALSE (default), print cli progress bar falling back per-location fetches. ... Forwarded edr_map() returning map. instance_id Optional collection instance identifier. supplied, capability planning every locations/data request use instance. keyword-argument leaves existing positional calls unchanged.

title: One-shot fetch + plot + map for a collection — edr_explore
text: default method = "auto" picks cheapest route collection advertises data_queries: cube – one HTTP call returning CoverageCollection across whole bbox. Fast. Used collection supports cube bbox supplied. area – like cube uses polygon. Used coords supplied collection supports area. position – one HTTP call point. Useful vertical profiles returned position queries. per-location – fallback: one HTTP call per station via edr_location(). Slower (N+1), used neither spatial bulk query supported matching spatial input supplied. Force specific path setting method. coords required area position; method = "cube" bbox omitted, bbox derived returned locations.

title: Fetch data for multiple EDR locations — edr_location_batch
text: client edr_client(). collection_id Collection identifier. location_id non-empty character vector unique location ids. datetime Optional ISO-8601 instant interval. chunk = NULL, shared every request; otherwise bounded interval split per-request windows. Timestamp bounds normalized UTC calendar arithmetic; six fractional-second digits accepted R can preserve without loss. parameter_name Optional character vector parameter names shared every request. z Optional vertical level filter. crs Optional CRS URI response. format Either "covjson" (default) "csv". ... Additional query parameters forwarded every edr_location() request, limit. chunk Optional positive-integer calendar interval "1 day", "2 weeks", "1 month", "1 year". Requires bounded datetime interval. Month/year boundaries use anchored calendar arithmetic, January 31 start advances February 28/29 March 31. deduplicate TRUE (default), exact rows repeated different time windows location retained earliest request. Duplicates within one response, differing observations, rows different locations preserved. Ignored chunk NULL. max_requests Finite positive integer limiting number logical edr_location() calls complete plan. Transport-level retries increase count. Defaults 100. on_error Either "stop" (default), re-signals original condition immediately, "collect", records failures continues bounded request plan. progress TRUE, display cli progress bar multi-request batches. Defaults interactive(). instance_id Optional collection instance identifier. Every request remains beneath instance path.

title: Fetch data for multiple EDR locations — edr_location_batch
text: object class edr_location_batch edr_batch. contains requests, typed request-status tibble whose n_rows values describe raw responses cross-window deduplication; data, combined data tibble; errors, typed tibble collected conditions. object also records collection_id, instance_id, format.

what: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
title: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
text: Generally need call directly: high-level verbs (edr_locations(), edr_area(), etc.) build path query string . Use edr_request() need hit bespoke path non-standard parameter.

title: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
code: edr_request(   client,   path,   query = list(),   format = c("json", "geojson", "covjson", "csv", "html", "raw"),   parse = TRUE )

title: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
text: client edr_client edr_client(). path Path base URL (without leading slash), e.g. "collections/monitoring-locations/locations". query Named list query parameters. Values may scalars vectors; vectors joined ",". NULL entries dropped. format Response format: one "json" (default), "geojson", "covjson", "csv", "html", "raw". Passed ?f= (except "covjson", sent ?f=json CoverageJSON Accept hint, since EDR servers return CovJSON via JSON). parse TRUE (default), parses JSON / GeoJSON / CovJSON bodies R structures. FALSE, returns raw httr2 response.

title: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
text: parsed list, tibble, edr_response wrapper; httr2_response parse = FALSE; typed empty result HTTP 204 responses.

title: edr4r 0.3.0 (development version)
text: Add opt-, bounded GeoJSON pagination edr_locations() edr_items(). client follows server-advertised rel = "next" links, supports cursor- offset-based servers, refuses unsafe cross-origin continuations silent partial results configured caps. Add edr_location_batch() safe, finite, sequential multi-station pulls stable request/data/error tables, explicit station provenance, stop--collect failure handling. Add bounded calendar time-window planning edr_location_batch() station--window request caps, month-end/leap-year handling, normalized window provenance, optional exact boundary-row deduplication. Requests remain sequential existing retry throttling policy stays control. Reuse batch execution engine inside per-location edr_explore() preserving existing output summary-warning contract. Accept finite, safely representable numeric strings CoverageJSON ranges declare numeric dataType, currently emitted USGS waterdata, rejecting text, overflow/underflow, unsafe declared integers. Add edr_add_stations() composing one independently styled station groups existing coverage map, reusing interactive chart CSV popups. Coverage layers now render lower Leaflet pane, grid legends identify active parameter/unit, grid colour scales support identity, square-root, log1p transforms. Upgrade cross-endpoint Lake Mead vignette precomputed interactive map: Met Office population Grid beneath toggleable USGS USBR/WWDH station groups time-series popups. full widget deployed pkgdown site; package builds retain small static fallback. Remove precomputed base64 map USGS vignette, reducing source file 1 MB retaining runnable interactive-map example.

title: edr4r 0.2.0 (development version)
text: Add structured service collection capability discovery, support checks, endpoint diagnostics, per-client TTL caching discovery metadata. Add collection-instance discovery optional instance-scoped paths across query verbs edr_explore(). Add documented compatibility contract plus frozen Met Office Labs metadata/terrain fixtures deterministic cross-server tests.

title: edr4r 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-10 Harden CoverageJSON parsing: honor NdArray$dataType, preserve textual identifiers invalid/mixed timestamps, validate array shape axes, support tuple/composite trajectory axes, merge child parameter metadata, fail clearly tiled externally referenced data. Return typed empty results HTTP 204 responses, report single Coverage Feature documents correctly, reject JSON error bodies CSV requested. Retry transient transport failures default, client option disable , exercise retries real local HTTP server. Make corridor_height required, validate radius/corridor distances units, reject non-finite reversed bounding boxes consistently. Make edr_explore(method = "auto") choose bulk spatial queries matching spatial input supplied. Capability discovery failures now stop accidental per-station request fan-. Add max_requests safety cap per-location exploration, preserve station identity combined plots, avoid optional location discovery calls grid/profile data-bulk results. Add grid vertical-profile plot views edr_plot(), automatic view detection. Allow edr_explore() return plots data gridded/profile coverage queries, including method = "position" profile-style responses. Add interactive coverage maps edr_map() edr_explore(), -map selectors gridded coverage slices (parameter, datetime, z) profile selectors parameter / datetime. Materialize CoverageJSON regular-grid axes declared start, stop, num. Warn per-station edr_explore() fallback fetches fail. Tighten collection-id, coordinate, WKT validation. Add max_match_distance edr_map() bounded spatial matching. Fix map filtering matching blank JavaScript values remain missing, requested parameters determine station availability, coverage groups assigned station retained. Run R CMD check coverage workflows dev branch well release branch. Add real headless-Chrome smoke test coverage-map controls, redraws, popups, uncaught JavaScript errors. Document non-operational Met Office Labs EDR demonstrator probe one tiny terrain query weekly/manual, non-blocking interoperability job.

title: edr4r 0.1.0

Query verbs: edr_locations() / edr_location(), edr_items() / edr_item(), edr_position(), edr_area(), edr_cube(), edr_radius(), edr_trajectory(), edr_corridor(). Low-level escape hatch: edr_request().
